chunk_summary: This section discusses food labeling thresholds and system pitfalls in India, focusing on regulatory frameworks, challenges in implementation, and their impact on food allergy management and prevention.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of food labeling regulations in managing food allergies in India.
Identify the challenges and pitfalls in implementing food labeling systems.
Analyze the impact of referral thresholds on food allergy prevention and treatment.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current Indian regulatory frameworks for food labeling.
key_facts: The Food Safety and Standards Authority of India (FSSAI) regulates food labeling under the 2025 Labelling and Display Regulations.
Referral thresholds for allergen labeling are critical but inconsistently implemented in India.
Cow’s milk protein allergy (CMPA) is a significant concern, with consensus guidelines published in 2020 by Indian Pediatrics.
Anaphylaxis management guidelines are provided by the Indian Academy of Pediatrics (IAP) in their Standard Treatment Guidelines.
Eosinophilic esophagitis in children is addressed in ESPGHAN’s 2024 guidelines, highlighting diagnostic and management protocols.
reference_quote: The Food Safety and Standards (Labelling and Display) Regulations, 2025, emphasize the need for clear allergen labeling to prevent adverse reactions in sensitive individuals. (Source: FSSAI)
document_key_topics: Management of cow’s milk protein allergy
